subject: [PATCH v2 10/18] block: convert iomap ops to ->iomap_next()

reviews.author: Keith Busch
reviews.summary: Found the rest of the series context on linux-fsdevel mailing list and reviewed the patch as good to go.
reviews.raw_body:
On Tue, Jun 30, 2026 at 05:09:25PM -0700, Joanne Koong wrote:
>  static const struct iomap_ops blkdev_iomap_ops = {
> -	.iomap_begin		= blkdev_iomap_begin,
> +	.iomap_next		= blkdev_iomap_next,
>  };

I think it's generally safe to use the same mailing list for the entire
series. There's no context here on what "iomap_next" is because I'm
subscribed only to linux-block. I found the rest here:

  https://lore.kernel.org/linux-fsdevel/20260701000949.1666714-1-joannelkoong@gmail.com/

FWIW, everything looks good to me.

Reviewed-by: Keith Busch <kbusch@kernel.org>

2026-06-30.patch_summary: This patch series converts the block iomap operations to use the new ->iomap_next() callback, which is processed by the iomap_process() helper.

reviews.author: Christoph Hellwig
reviews.summary: Agreed with Keith that some people might miss the context without the linux-fsdevel link.
reviews.raw_body:
On Tue, Jun 30, 2026 at 06:55:22PM -0600, Keith Busch wrote:

> 
> I think it's generally safe to use the same mailing list for the entire
> series. There's no context here on what "iomap_next" is because I'm
> subscribed only to linux-block. I found the rest here:

Yeah, without that some people always get screwed over reviewing the
series.

> FWIW, everything looks good to me.
>

Same here:

Reviewed-by: Christoph Hellwig <hch@lst.de>

reviews.author: Joanne Koong
reviews.summary: Acknowledged Keith's suggestion to include the linux-fsdevel link in future patches.
reviews.raw_body:
On Tue, Jun 30, 2026 at 5:55 PM Keith Busch <kbusch@kernel.org> wrote:
>
> On Tue, Jun 30, 2026 at 05:09:25PM -0700, Joanne Koong wrote:
> >  static const struct iomap_ops blkdev_iomap_ops = {
> > -     .iomap_begin            = blkdev_iomap_begin,
> > +     .iomap_next             = blkdev_iomap_next,
> >  };
>
> I think it's generally safe to use the same mailing list for the entire
> series. There's no context here on what "iomap_next" is because I'm
> subscribed only to linux-block. I found the rest here:
>
>   https://lore.kernel.org/linux-fsdevel/20260701000949.1666714-1-joannelkoong@gmail.com/

Ahh I see, thanks for the heads up Keith. I'll do that from now on.

Thanks,
Joanne

2026-07-02.patch_summary: This patch series converts the block iomap operations to use the new ->iomap_next() callback, which is processed by the iomap_process() helper.
